summary: Added shared JetBrains/PyCharm project metadata under .idea for the melee repository. The PR committed project/module configuration, VCS mapping, inspection profile settings, and a scoped .idea gitignore that keeps local workspace, shelf, HTTP request, and datasource state out of version control.
what_changed: Created .idea/.gitignore with JetBrains-local ignored paths: shelf, workspace.xml, httpRequests, dataSources, and dataSources.local.xml.
Added .idea/melee.iml as a PyCharm PYTHON_MODULE using the repository root as content and excluding $MODULE_DIR$/venv.
Configured the module to use an inherited JDK and a non-test sourceFolder order entry.
Added PyDocumentationSettings in melee.iml with plain documentation/docstring formatting.
Added .idea/misc.xml with ProjectRootManager set to Python SDK name "Python 3.10 (melee)".
Added .idea/modules.xml registering .idea/melee.iml as the project module.
smart_moves: Committed only shareable PyCharm project metadata while explicitly ignoring volatile per-user IDE files such as workspace.xml, shelf data, HTTP requests, and datasource storage.
Excluded the local venv directory from the PyCharm module so IDE indexing and project navigation avoid common generated/local environment content.
Used JetBrains path macros such as $PROJECT_DIR$ and $MODULE_DIR$ instead of absolute local paths, making the metadata portable across checkouts.
decomp_lessons: Shared editor metadata can be useful when it standardizes Python tooling around repository scripts, but it should avoid committing user-specific or machine-specific state.
For decompilation repositories with Python maintenance scripts, IDE configuration should treat virtual environments and local runtime artifacts as excluded/ignored content.
When adding IDE metadata, prefer portable project-relative macros and narrow .gitignore rules inside the IDE metadata directory.
naming_conventions: PyCharm module file was named .idea/melee.iml, matching the repository/project name.

JetBrains XML component names follow IDE-generated conventions such as ProjectModuleManager, ProjectRootManager, VcsDirectoryMappings, NewModuleRootManager, PyDocumentationSettings, and InspectionProjectProfileManager.
The configured Python SDK display name was "Python 3.10 (melee)".
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided slice, so there is no evidence of reviewer rationale or requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or remove .idea project metadata.
Search for repository policy discussions around committing JetBrains IDE files.
Check whether Python tooling configuration later moved to pyproject.toml, requirements files, or editor-agnostic settings.
